nodes.label: textEditBox
nodes.defaultValue: The VoiceTextEditBox associated with the keyboard

nodes.label: keyGrid
nodes.defaultValue: The DynamicKeyGrid associated with the keyboard

nodes.label: command
nodes.access: Specifies the command to be executed:getUserDatagetUserRegionDatagetCatalog and getStoreCatalogdoOrdergetPurchases and getAllPurchasesstoreChannelCredDatagetChannelCredgetDeviceAttestationTokenrequestPartnerOrderconfirmPartnerOrder

nodes.label: context
nodes.access:
Specifies the context of the RFI screen, which may be one of the following values:
      "signup": The RFI screen displays a "Let's create your account" title and lists the customer information specified in the requestedUserData field. The RFI screen uses the "signup" context by default. See Sign-up requirements and best practices for more information on implementing the app sign-up UI."signin: "The RFI screen displays a "Sign in" title and lists only email or phone attributes, if specified in the requestedUserData field. Other attributes are ignored, even if specified. See the Sign-in example for how to use this field. See Sign-in requirements and best practices for more information on implementing the app sign-in UI.

nodes.label: forceShowData
nodes.access: If true, the RFI signup screen displays the values of the requested customer information to be shared with the app (for example, Jone Doe, [jon.doe@emailaddress.com](mailto:jon.doe@emailaddress.com)).By default, this flag is set to false, which means that the default RFI screen for the region is used. For example, in the US, the RFI screen displays the type of customer information being requested (email address, name, and so on).This flag has no effect if the context field is set to "signin" (the RFI sign-in screen always displays the customer information values). Example:store = CreateObject("roSGNode", "ChannelStore")' Doesn't show user data in dialog unless necessary in the user's region. store.requestedUserData = "email,firstname,lastname,gender,birth"store.command = "getUserData"' Shows user data in dialog.info = CreateObject("roSGNode", "ContentNode")info.addFields(\{forceShowData: true})store.requestedUserDataInfo = infostore.requestedUserData = "email"store.command = "getUserData"

nodes.label: action
nodes.access: Set this to "Upgrade" or "Downgrade" to change the subscription plan from a previous purchase (for example, myOrder.action = "Upgrade"). The required values are case-sensitive; do not pass "upgrade" or "downgrade". See On-device upgrade and downgrade for more information.

nodes.label: keyGrid
nodes.defaultValue: The DynamicKeyGrid node associated with the keyboard

nodes.label: focusBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list has focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions as well as margins. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: focusFootprintBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list does not have focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions as well as margins. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: wrapDividerBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file to use as a wrap divider, the visual separator between the last and first list items when the list wraps. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: sectionDividerBitmapUri
nodes.access: If the ContentNode specifies sections for a list or grid, specifies a custom bitmap to use as a visual divider between the sections of the list or grid. Only set this field to use a bitmap with a different appearance than the system default. For sections that do not include an icon or a title, the system default or custom bitmap specified as the wrapDividerBitmapUri field value is used for the section dividers. In most cases, you will want to use a 9-patch PNG bitmap with both expandable regions, which is the type of bitmap used as the system default.

nodes.label: itemComponentName
nodes.access: Specifies the name of a XML component for the grid items. An instance of this component is created on demand for each visible item of the grid. The XML component must define a specific interface as detailed in MarkupGrid XML component below.

nodes.label: imageWellBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file to use to suggest where images would appear for empty grids and empty sections of grids. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: focusBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list has focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions as well as margins. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: focusFootprintBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list does not have focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions as well as margins. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: wrapDividerBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file to use as a wrap divider, the visual separator between the last and first list items when the list wraps. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: sectionDividerBitmapUri
nodes.access: If the ContentNode specifies sections for a list or grid, specifies a custom bitmap to use as a visual divider between the sections of the list or grid. Only set this field to use a bitmap with a different appearance than the system default. For sections that do not include an icon or a title, the system default or custom bitmap specified as the wrapDividerBitmapUri field value is used for the section dividers. In most cases, you will want to use a 9-patch PNG bitmap with both expandable regions, which is the type of bitmap used as the system default.

nodes.label: width
nodes.access: Set to the width of the list item.

nodes.label: height
nodes.access: Set to the height of the list item

nodes.label: index
nodes.access: Set to the index of this item in the data model.

nodes.label: listHasFocus
nodes.access: Set to true if the **MarkupList** node has focus, false otherwise.

nodes.label: itemContent
nodes.access: Contains the data to be displayed by the list item. The relationship between data in the **ContentNode** node and the visual elements of the list item is determined by the scripts in the item XML component. Typically, an observer of the `itemContent` field is used to update the list item when the content changes.

nodes.label: focusPercent
nodes.access: The fractional value, from 0.0 to 1.0, of a time delay after focus has moved from one item to the next. The fractional value increases incrementally from 0.0 to 1.0 for the newly-focused item, while simultaneously decreasing from 1.0 to 0.0 for the previously-focused item. This value can be used as a timing key to smoothly animate the appearance of the focused item as well as the previously-focused item, to indicate the movement of focus to the user.

nodes.label: itemHasFocus
nodes.access: Indicates whether the item component currently is the MarkupList's focused item. When scrolling starts, the itemHasFocus field for the currently focused item is set to false. When scrolling ends, the itemHasFocus field for the newly focused item is set to true. During the scrolling animation, all itemHasFocus fields are set to false.Only one item component of any MarkupList should have itemHasFocus set to true. If the MarkupList does not focus, all itemHasFocus fields of their item components should be set to false.

nodes.label: HDGRIDPOSTERURL
nodes.defaultValue: The image file for the icon to be displayed to the left of the section label when the screen resolution is set to HD.

nodes.label: itemComponentName
nodes.access: Specifies the name of a XML component for the list items. An instance of this component is created on demand for each visible item of the list. The XML component must define a specific interface as detailed in MarkupList XML component below.

nodes.label: focusBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list has focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions as well as margins. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: focusFootprintBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list does not have focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions as well as margins. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: wrapDividerBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file to use as a wrap divider, the visual separator between the last and first list items when the list wraps. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: sectionDividerBitmapUri
nodes.access: If the ContentNode specifies sections for a list or grid, specifies a custom bitmap to use as a visual divider between the sections of the list or grid. Only set this field to use a bitmap with a different appearance than the system default. For sections that do not include an icon or a title, the system default or custom bitmap specified as the wrapDividerBitmapUri field value is used for the section dividers. In most cases, you will want to use a 9-patch PNG bitmap with both expandable regions, which is the type of bitmap used as the system default.

nodes.label: imageWellBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file to use to suggest where images would appear for empty grids and empty sections of grids. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: loadingBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies a bitmap file to display while a grid item's poster is loading.To execute a seamless cross-fade transition between posters, set the loadingBitmapUri of the next poster to be shown to the uri of the currently displayed poster.

nodes.label: failedBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies a bitmap file to display when a grid item poster fails to load

nodes.label: captionBackgroundBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies a bitmap file to render as a background for the grid item captions

nodes.label: focusBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list has focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions as well as margins. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: focusFootprintBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list does not have focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies both expandable regions as well as margins. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: sectionDividerBitmapUri
nodes.access: If the ContentNode specifies sections for a list or grid, specifies a custom bitmap to use as a visual divider between the sections of the list or grid. Only set this field to use a bitmap with a different appearance than the system default. For sections that do not include an icon or a title, the system default or custom bitmap specified as the wrapDividerBitmapUri field value is used for the section dividers. In most cases, you will want to use a 9-patch PNG bitmap with both expandable regions, which is the type of bitmap used as the system default.

nodes.label: TITLE
nodes.defaultValue: The name of this channel, to be visible only if HDSMALLICONURL is not set or if the icon failed to load.

nodes.label: showPastTimeScreen
nodes.access: If true, enables drawing a solid color background behind all the programs in the grid airing before the current time.

nodes.label: pastTimeScreenBlendColor
nodes.access: Tints the past time screen by multiplying the color of each pixel by the specified value. If this value is not set to a value other than the default value, no color blending will be performed

nodes.label: pastTimeScreenBitmapUri
nodes.access: Displays the specified bitmap file in the background of the past time screen.

nodes.label: nowNextMode
nodes.access: When set to true, the time grid displays two vertical columns of programs showing Now and Next for each channel

nodes.label: nowBackgroundBitmapUri
nodes.access: Displays the specified bitmap file in the background of the Now grid within the Now Bar

nodes.label: nextBackgroundBitmapUri
nodes.access: Displays the specified bitmap file in the background of the Next grid within the Now Bar

nodes.label: nowNextHideAmPm
nodes.access: Hides the AM/PM abbreviation next to the times in the Now Bar.

nodes.label: programNowNextTimeColor
nodes.access: If Now/Next mode is true, specifies the text color for the time that appears in the now/next program cells when the program is unfocused

nodes.label: programNowNextTimeFocusedColor
nodes.access: If Now/Next mode is true, specifies the text color for the time that appears in the Now/Next program cells when the program is focused

nodes.label: programNowNextHorizMargin
nodes.access: Specifies the width of the left and right margins of program cell text in Now/Next mode.

nodes.label: programNowNextTimeTitleGap
nodes.access: Specifies the gap between the **Now** and **Next** entries

nodes.label: maxDays
nodes.access: Specifies the total width of the time grid in days.

nodes.label: animateToChannel
nodes.access: Write-only. Scrolls the grid so the row corresponding to the specified app index is on screen. When the scrolling ends, the program in that row at the current focus time will have focus.

nodes.label: jumpToChannel
nodes.access: Write-only. Jumps the grid so the row corresponding to the specified app index is on screen. After the jump, the program in that row at the current focus time will have focus.

nodes.label: jumpToTime
nodes.access: Write-only. Jumps the grid to the program at the specified time (in UTC format). After the jump, the time will be visible, and the program on the currently focused app will have focus.

nodes.label: channelSelected
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the selected app when the user makes a selection.

nodes.label: channelFocused
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the focused app when a program in the app's row gains focus.

nodes.label: channelUnfocused
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the focused app when a program in the app's row loses focus.

nodes.label: channelInfoSelected
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the selected app when the user selects an item in the Channel Information column.

nodes.label: channelInfoFocused
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the app when an app in the Channel Information column gains focus.

nodes.label: channelInfoUnfocused
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the app when an app in the Channel Information column loses focus.

nodes.label: channelInfoFocusable
nodes.access: Indicates whether the user can move focus into the Channel Information column. If this field is false, the Channel Info column in non-interactive.

nodes.label: channelInfoWidth
nodes.access: Width of the column showing the app names.

nodes.label: infoGridGap
nodes.access: Spacing between the Channel Information column and the main grid.

nodes.label: programTitleFocusedColor
nodes.access: Specifies the text color for the program title that is focused.

nodes.label: programTitleColor
nodes.access: Specifies the text color for program titles that are unfocused.

nodes.label: programTitleFont
nodes.access: Specifies the font for the program titles

nodes.label: programSelected
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the program in the currently focused channel row when the user selects a program.

nodes.label: programFocused
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the program in the currently focused channel row when the program gains focus.

nodes.label: programUnfocused
nodes.access: Read-only. Indicates the index of the program in the currently focused channel row when the program loses focus.

nodes.label: jumpToProgram
nodes.access: Write-only. Jumps the grid immediately to the program corresponding to the specified program index for the currently focused row. After jumping, the program will be visible and have focus.

nodes.label: programHorizMargin
nodes.access: Specifies the width of the left and right margins of program cell text.

nodes.label: fillProgramGaps
nodes.access: Inserts a program with the "No Data Available" label if there is a gap between the program's start time and the previous program's end time

nodes.label: loadingDataText
nodes.access: The text to be displayed over the program grid whenever the loading message is to be shown.

nodes.label: channelNoDataText
nodes.access: Displays the specified text on each channel row if no data exists for a specific time (or if there is a gap in the program data).

nodes.label: timeBarHeight
nodes.access: Specifies the height of the region above the app grid where the time labels are displayed.

nodes.label: timeLabelColor
nodes.access: Specifies the text color for the time labels that are displayed in the time bar at the top of the TimeGrid.

nodes.label: timeLabelFont
nodes.access: Specifies the font for the time labels that are displayed in the time bar at the top of the TimeGrid.

nodes.label: timeLabelOffset
nodes.access: Specifies the offset from the edge of the grid of the leftmost time label.

nodes.label: nowBarWidth
nodes.access: Specifies the width of the Now Bar

nodes.label: nowBarHeight
nodes.access: Specifies the height of the Now Bar

nodes.label: nowBarBlendColor
nodes.access: Tints the Now Bar by multiplying the color of each pixel by the specified value. If this value is not set to a value other than the default value, no color blending will be performed.

nodes.label: nowBarOffset
nodes.access: Specifies the number of pixels the left edge of the Now Bar is offset from the current time.

nodes.label: contentStartTime
nodes.access: The earliest time to which the time grid can be scrolled. The scrollable range of times is defined by the sum of the contentStartTime and maxDays fields.

nodes.label: duration
nodes.access: Width (in seconds) of the visible section of the time grid.For example, setting this to 10800 will cause the time grid to display 3 hours of programs horizontally (10,800 = 3 hours \* 3,600 seconds per hour).

nodes.label: autoDismissTime
nodes.access: Specifies the time (in seconds) that the time grid will be displayed before automatically being hidden. Setting this field to 0 will cause the time grid to never be hidden.

nodes.label: ignoreTrickPlayKeys
nodes.access: If set to true, ignores fast forward, rewind, and instant replay key events.Fast forward and rewind key presses normally page the time grid up and down. Instant replay key presses jump the grid to the current time.

nodes.label: overlayBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies an overlay that is drawn on top of the entire time grid. This is used by default to make the bottom visible rows of the time grid appear to fade out, but could be used to add any overlay be drawn on top of the time grid. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies expandable regions.Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap. The overlay bitmap is drawn over the full width of the time grid with its bottom aligned to the bottom of the time grid.If the overlayHeight field value is greater than 0 the image will be stretched to the specified height (that is from the bottom of the time grid to the specified height).If overlayHeight field value is set to 0 the bitmap will be drawn at its default height.

nodes.label: overlayHeight
nodes.access: Specifies the height of the overlay image (see the overlayBitmapUri field). If set to 0 the overlay image is drawn at its default height.

nodes.label: focusBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the focus indicator when the list has focus. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies expandable regions. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: leftEdgeTargetTime
nodes.access: When explicitly set, this triggers a horizontally scrolling animation so that the specified time is displayed at the left edge of the program grid.This field is also set by the firmware in response to remote control key presses that cause horizontal scrolling to provide feedback on the currently visible time range.

nodes.label: channelInfoComponentName
nodes.access: Uses the specified RSG component to display the data for each channel in the Channel Information column.An instance of this component is created on demand for each channel. The component must define a specific interface. Specifically, the content field of this component can be used to configure the data for the app information column (see [Specifying list and grid content](doc:list-and-grid-nodes#specifying-list-and-grid-content) for more information).Additionally, observer functions of the optional width, height, and hasFocus fields of the custom component can be used to customize the appearance (see [Custom item definitions and focus indicators](doc:overview#custom-item-definitions-and-focus-indicators) for more information). If this field is not specified, the default channel info will be displayed, which is an optional channel icon followed by a label containing the name of the app. The icon and label come from the app ContentNodes as described in the [Channel Attributes](doc:timegrid#fields) section.

nodes.label: jumpToChannelInfo
nodes.access: Write-only. Jumps the grid immediately to the app info column box of the specified channel index. The app row will be visible, and the app info box for the specified channel will have focus. If the channelInfoFocusable field is false, this field is ignored.

nodes.label: channelInfoColumnLabel
nodes.access: Specifies text used as a label for the header of the Channel Information column (the text displayed above the Channel Information column).If this is not specified, the label will show the day of the week that the left-hand edge of the time grid represents.

nodes.label: channelInfoTextColor
nodes.access: Specifies the text color for the app names in the Channel Information column if the channelInfoComponentName field is not specified.

nodes.label: channelInfoFont
nodes.access: Specifies the font for the app names if the channelInfoComponentName field is not specified.

nodes.label: channelInfoBackgroundBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file to use as the background for the app names in the grid if the channelInfoComponentName field is not specified. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies expandable regions. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: programBackgroundBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file to use as the background for the program names in the grid. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies expandable regions. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: automaticLoadingDataFeedback
nodes.access: Enables the program data region of the grid to be automatically replaced with the loading message specified in the loadingDataText field whenever the content field has not been set or the user scrolls to a time where the content has not yet been loaded.

nodes.label: showLoadingDataFeedback
nodes.access: Replaces the program data region of the grid with the loading message specified in the loadingDataText field. This field enables you to explicitly control when the loading message is displayed instead of it being automatically shown when automaticLoadingDataFeedbackis enabled.This field is ignored if automaticLoadingDataFeedbackis true.

nodes.label: timeBarBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file to use as the background of the time bar that appears above the time grid channel grid. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies expandable regions. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: nowBarBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the bitmap file used for the Now Bar. In most cases, this should be a 9-patch image that specifies expandable regions. Only set this field to specify a custom bitmap that differs in appearance from the default bitmap.

nodes.label: minimumNowBarOffset
nodes.access: Specifies (in seconds) the nearest that the Now Bar can be to the left-hand edge of the time grid. If the Now Bar advances to the minimumNowBarOffset field value plus 30 minutes the time grid will automatically scroll right 30 minutes.

nodes.label: errorMsg
nodes.access: An error message describing the audio play error set in the state field.

nodes.label: drm\_lic\_acq\_dur
nodes.access: License acquisition. This typically includes interactions with the license server.

nodes.label: manifest\_start
nodes.access: Point at which manifest download and parsing begins.

nodes.label: drm\_load\_start
nodes.access: Point at which DRM system initialization begins.

nodes.label: drm\_lic\_acq\_start
nodes.access: Point at which license acquisition begins.

nodes.label: prebuf\_start
nodes.access: Point at which content pre-buffering begins.

nodes.label: icon
nodes.access: A 9-patch or PNG of the icon to be displayed when the button does not have.

nodes.label: focusIcon
nodes.access: A 9-patch or PNG of the icon to be displayed when the button has focus.

nodes.label: buttonIsDisabled
nodes.access: Controls whether the button is disabled (true) or enabled (false). A disabled button is skipped and does not have focus while the user navigates the different playback action buttons with the directional pad on the Roku remote control.

nodes.label: htiles
nodes.access: Horizontal number of thumbnails in a tile (columns.)

nodes.label: vtiles
nodes.access: Vertical number of thumbnails in a tile (rows.)

nodes.label: width
nodes.access: Number of horizontal pixels in a thumbnail (this is not the tile as the one in the DASH spec).

nodes.label: height
nodes.access: Number of vertical pixels in a thumbnail (this is not the same tile as the one in the DASH spec).

nodes.label: bandwidth
nodes.access: Max tile size in bits / duration.

nodes.label: final\_time
nodes.access: End time of current tile\_set in seconds.

nodes.label: frameBgBlendColor
nodes.access: A color to be blended with the image displayed behind individual BIF images displayed on the screen. The blending is performed by multiplying this value with each pixel in the image. If not changed from the default value, no blending will take place.

nodes.label: frameBgImageUri
nodes.access: The URI of an image to be displayed behind individual frames on the screen. The actual frame image is displayed opaquely on top of this background, so only the outer edges of this image are visible. Because of that, this background image typically appears as a border around the video frame. If the frameBgBlendColor field is set to a value other than the default, that color will be blended with the background image.

nodes.label: currentTimeMarkerBlendColor
nodes.access: This is blended with the marker for the current playback position. This is typically a small vertical bar displayed in the TrickPlayBar node when the user is fast-forwarding or rewinding through the video.

nodes.label: textColor
nodes.access: Sets the color of the text next to the trickPlayBar node indicating the time elapsed/remaining.

nodes.label: thumbBlendColor
nodes.access: Sets the blend color of the square image in the trickPlayBar node that shows the current position, with the current direction arrows or pause icon on top. The blending is performed by multiplying this value with each pixel in the image. If not changed from the default value, no blending will take place.

nodes.label: percentage
nodes.access: The percentage of the work that is done. Setting this field controls the visual appearance of the ProgressBar node.

nodes.label: ServiceLocation
nodes.access: A blacklist of failed BaseURL locations.

nodes.label: errorMsg
nodes.access: An error message describing the video play error set in the state field.Use the errorStr and and errorInfo fields for more descriptive diagnostic information to help identify and resolve the cause of the error.

nodes.label: initial\_time
nodes.access: Presentation start time of current tile\_set in seconds. Thumbnails in tiles beginning before this time should be skipped, and the first relevant thumbnail duration should be updated accordingly.

nodes.label: tiles
nodes.access: Contains information about each tile in the tile\_set. This contains the following fields: $\{tiles-list}

nodes.label: getNearestFrame
nodes.access: Write-OnlyRequests the nearest BIF to the time specified. This would normally be an offset from the current playback position. The getNearestFrame request is passed to the BifCache which uses the getNearestFrame() method implemented on all BIF storage classes. Existing BifCache functionality is then used to retrieve the bitmap data and load it into the texture manager.

nodes.label: nearestFrame
nodes.access: Read-OnlyContains the URI of the requested BIF. The returned URIs will be of the form 'memory://BIF_%d_%d'. These URIs can then be used directly in the 'uri' field of a Poster SGN (or similar).

nodes.label: filledBarBlendColor
nodes.access: This color will be blended with the graphical image specified in the filledBarImageUri field. The blending is performed by multiplying this value with each pixel in the image. If not changed from the default value, no blending will take place.

nodes.label: liveFilledBarBlendColor
nodes.access: The color of the trickplay progress bar to be blended with the filledBarImageUri for live linear streams.

nodes.label: filledBarImageUri
nodes.access: A 9-patch or ordinary PNG of the bar that represents the completed portion of the work represented by this ProgressBar node. This is typically displayed on the left side of the track. This will be blended with the color specified by the filledBarBlendColor field, if set to a non-default value.

nodes.label: trackBlendColor
nodes.access: This color is blended with the graphical image specified by trackImageUri field. The blending is performed by multiplying this value with each pixel in the image. If not changed from the default value, no blending will take place.

nodes.label: trackImageUri
nodes.access: A 9-patch or ordinary PNG of the track of the progress bar, which surrounds the filled and empty bars. This will be blended with the color specified by the trackBlendColor field, if set to a non-default value.

nodes.label: emptyBarBlendColor
nodes.access: A color to be blended with the graphical image specified in the emptyBarImageUri field. The blending is performed by multiplying this value with each pixel in the image. If not changed from the default value, no blending will take place.

nodes.label: emptyBarImageUri
nodes.access: A 9-patch or ordinary PNG of the bar presenting the remaining work to be done. This is typically displayed on the right side of the track, and is blended with the color specified in the emptyBarBlendColor field, if set to a non-default value.

nodes.label: subtitleTrack
nodes.access: The identifier of the selected subtitle track. Subtitles may or may not be visible on the screen, depending upon the user's caption mode setting. Reading this field will return the identifier of the subtitle track selected by the user. Writing this the field will change the track.See also: globalCaptionMode

nodes.label: currentSubtitleTrack
nodes.access: The identifier of the selected subtitle track. Subtitles may or may not be visible on the screen, depending upon the user's caption mode setting. Reading this field will return the identifier of the subtitle track that is playing. When the user has not selected a track, the Roku media player will select a track based on the preferred caption language system setting.

nodes.label: audioTrack
nodes.access: The track identifier of the selected audio track. Reading this field will return the track identifier of the audio selected by the user.Writing this value will change the audio track. However,  apps should not do this unless they are implementing  their own track selection menu that users control. This is because the Roku OS selects the best track automatically based on preferred language setting on the device. See Automatic audio track selection for more information.

nodes.label: currentAudioTrack
nodes.access: The track identifier of the audio being played. Reading this field will return the track that is being played, which may be different than the track being selected (for example, when the Roku media player cannot play a certain format). When the user has not selected an audio track, the platform will select a track based on the preferred audio language setting.

nodes.label: URLFilter
nodes.access: A substring that identifies the (base)URL to which these CDN settings apply. The Roku media player matches this string against all (base)URLs listed in the manifest and applies the setting to all (base)URLs that contain this substring.

nodes.label: ContentFilter
nodes.access: For DASH streams, a substring that filters the period or asset ID to which these CDN settings apply. The Roku player only applies these CDN setting to periods with a period ID or asset ID that contains this substring. This match is used in addition to the URL filter.

nodes.label: Priority
nodes.access: For configuring failovers, sets the priority for this (base)URL from 1 to x (a priority of 0 or less is invalid). A lower value indicates a higher priority. For example, a (base)URL with a priority of 1 is higher than another with a priority of 10. If the highest priority server fails, traffic is routed to the server with the next highest priority. If all servers are configured with the same priority, and one fails, no failover will happen.

nodes.label: Weight
nodes.access: For configuring load balancing, sets the relative weight for all (base)URLs with the same priority. This must be a value of 1 or greater (a weight of 0 disables a CDN). The weight of a given BaseURL is its weight value divided by the sum of all weight values. This means that to spread the load equally across multiple CDNs with the same priority, set the weight for each to the same value. To configure the weights for two servers to 80% and a third server to 20%, for example, set servers one and two to 8 and server three to 4.

nodes.label: uri
nodes.access: Specifies the URI of the image file. Images included as part of the application package can be referenced using the pkg:/images prefix. Images included as part of the application package that are to be localized can be referenced using the pkg:/locale/images/ prefix (see Localizing Graphical Images in the Application Package)

nodes.label: loadingBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies a bitmap file to display while the poster image is loading.To execute a seamless cross-fade transition between posters, set the loadingBitmapUri of the next poster to be shown to the uri of the currently displayed poster.

nodes.label: failedBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies a bitmap file to display when the poster image failed to load

nodes.label: audioGuideText
nodes.access: If muteAudioGuide is false, this string will be spoken when the poster is focused

nodes.label: color
nodes.fieldType: color (string containing hex value e.g. RGBA)

nodes.label: text
nodes.access: Specifies the text to be displayed

nodes.label: fontSize
nodes.access: Specifies the size of the font in points. As noted in the description of the fontUri field, the use of fixed size system fonts ignores the value of the fontSize field.

nodes.label: pin
nodes.access: Contains the string of numbers that have been entered.

nodes.label: pinLength
nodes.access: Contains the maximum number of digits that can be entered

nodes.label: secureMode
nodes.access: When set to true, each digit entered is displayed briefly, then replaced with an asterisk. When false, the entered digits always remain visible.

nodes.label: keyColor
nodes.access: Specifies the color of the key labels and icons when the keyboard does not have the focus

nodes.label: focusedKeyColor
nodes.access: Specifies the color of the key labels and icons when the keyboard has the focus

nodes.label: pinDisplayTextColor
nodes.access: Specifies the color of the numbers displayed in the pin display boxes

nodes.label: keyboardBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the URI of an image file to be loaded to replace the default keyboard image drawn underneath the numeric keys and icons. Note that this image must be carefully designed so that the key positions match the default image. Template images for SD, HD and FHD resolutions are provided below.

nodes.label: pinDisplayBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifed the URI of an image file to be loaded to replace the default box drawn underneath each entered digit in the pin display. This should be a 9-patch image so that it can be stretched to appropriate size depending on the pinLength field.

nodes.label: focusBitmapUri
nodes.access: Specifies the URI of an image file to be loaded to replace the keyboard focus indicator. This should be a 9-patch image so that it can be stretched to the appropriate size for the double width keys.

nodes.label: pinSuccess
nodes.access:
Read-only
        
        
          "true": Content is now unblocked
          "false": Pin incorrect, "incomplete": a full pin is not entered

nodes.label: showPinDisplay
nodes.access: Specifies whether or not the pin display that shows the entered digits is visible. In most cases, it is desirable to display the entered digits so that the user can see the string as it is entered. In some cases though, you might want to only show the keyboard part of the PinPad node.In those cases, the pinfield of the node will still contain the string entered by the user, so that it can displayed in some different manner.
